steps.description: NIST 800-88 software erasure or physical destruction. Individual certificate per asset with verification hash.

steps.title: Refurbishment
steps.description: Complete diagnostic, upgrade and compliance testing. Eligible equipment is prepared for a second life.

hero.title: Why GreenTechCycle?
hero.subtitle: 50 million tonnes of WEEE per year in Europe, with 60% of IT scope 3 concentrated in upstream manufacturing. GreenTechCycle was born from a conviction: ITAD must become a line item in corporate accounting.

mission.title: Making ITAD a line item in corporate accounting
paragraphs: Every year, 50 million tonnes of WEEE are generated in Europe. 60% of corporate IT scope 3 is concentrated in upstream manufacturing (source: Boavizta). Extending the useful life of an IT asset from 5 to 9 years reduces its unit carbon footprint by 45%.

Our mission: make ITAD a measurable, auditable, and optimizable accounting entry -- on par with the electricity bill. Our 2030 vision: 80% of French B2B IT fleet in short-circuit refurbishment, with real-time scope 3 traceability.

founder.title: An engineer who came through banking cybersecurity
founder.quote: In 2019, I watched 2,000 hard drives being shredded without a single certificate in a bank datacenter. The invoice was paid, the risk was real, and nobody had proof. That day, I decided to create GreenTechCycle.
founder.bio: Graduate engineer, 15 years of experience including 8 as CISO in banking. CISSP certified, ISO 27001 Lead Auditor, Boavizta and INR member. GreenTechCycle was founded in Lyon in 2021.

items.title: Digital sovereignty
items.desc: No client data leaves European soil. OVHcloud SecNumCloud hosting, 100% EU subcontractors. No extra-European technology dependency.

items.title: Measured sobriety
items.desc: We publish our own scope 1+2+3 carbon report every year. SBTi 1.5C trajectory committed. Every decision is evaluated on its measurable carbon impact.

items.title: Social inclusion
items.desc: 27% of our technical staff comes from social-economy insertion programmes (IAE). Partnerships with Envie, Ateliers du Bocage, Emmaus Connect. 41 IAE jobs supported in 2025.

items.title: Transparency and proof
items.desc: Every published number is sourced (ADEME, Boavizta, CIGREF). Every certificate is eIDAS-signed. Every operation is filmed, timestamped, archived for 10 years.

items.title: Operational excellence
items.desc: 38 employees, 12 R2v3 engineers, 4 certified CSRD auditors, 18 NIST 800-88 qualified technicians, 1 dedicated DPO. Contractual SLAs with penalties.

items.label: reuse rate (vs 32% standard WEEE pro circuit)

hero.title: Regulations
hero.subtitle: Understanding the obligations that impact your ITAD

timeline.name: CSRD
timeline.description: Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive — Mandatory extra-financial reporting including electronic waste management and circular economy.
timeline.itadImpact: Mandatory traceability of end-of-life IT assets, scope 3 emissions reporting for hardware.
timeline.sanctions: Financial penalties and executive liability for non-compliance.

timeline.name: NIS2
timeline.description: Network and Information Security Directive — Extended scope to mid-size companies with strengthened security obligations.
timeline.itadImpact: Mandatory certified erasure for essential and important entities. Data-related incident notification.
timeline.sanctions: Up to €10M or 2% of global turnover for essential entities.

timeline.name: DORA
timeline.description: Digital Operational Resilience Act — Digital operational resilience for the financial sector.
timeline.itadImpact: Risk management for ITAD providers. Regular audits and tests of destruction processes.
timeline.sanctions: Administrative sanctions up to 1% of average daily turnover.

timeline.name: AI Act
timeline.description: European AI Regulation — Framework for AI systems based on risk levels.
timeline.itadImpact: Lifecycle management of AI systems embedded in IT hardware. Training data traceability.
timeline.sanctions: Up to €35M or 7% of global turnover for the most serious violations.

hero.title: Security

items.name: Standard Deletion
items.desc: File deletion and quick format. Suitable for non-sensitive internal data.

items.name: Single Overwrite
items.desc: One overwrite pass with verification. For low-sensitivity data.

items.name: Multi-pass Overwrite
items.desc: 3 overwrite passes with alternating patterns and complete verification.

items.name: Cryptographic Erasure
items.desc: Encryption key destruction rendering data irrecoverable. Ideal for SSDs.

items.name: Physical Destruction
items.desc: Shredding, degaussing or certified incineration. For defense-classified data.

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions, organized by role

dsi.q: What erasure standards do you use?
dsi.a: We apply NIST 800-88 rev.1 (Clear, Purge, Destroy) and DoD 5220.22-M standards. Each operation generates an individual certificate with SHA-256 hash.

dsi.q: How do you guarantee chain of custody?
dsi.a: Every asset is tracked end-to-end: handover report, numbered seals, GPS transport, video-monitored processing and blockchain recording.

dsi.q: Is the platform compatible with our ITSM?
dsi.a: Yes, we offer native integrations with ServiceNow, GLPI, Intune and JAMF. Our REST API also enables custom integrations.
All data is hosted in France at OVHcloud (SecNumCloud qualification). AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit.
We guarantee 99.9% uptime SLA with 24/7 priority support for critical incidents.

dsi.q: How do you handle equipment under manufacturer warranty?
dsi.a: We systematically check warranty status and propose manufacturer return when relevant, before any ITAD processing.

dsi.q: Can you operate across multiple sites?
dsi.a: Yes, we operate across all of France and in 12 European countries. The platform natively supports multi-site management.

dsi.q: How does the platform RBAC work?
dsi.a: 5 predefined roles (Admin, Manager, Operator, Auditor, Reader) with 4-eyes principle for sensitive operations. Customization available.

rse.q: How do you calculate the carbon footprint?
rse.a: Via Boavizta API and ADEME emission factors. We cover scopes 1 (transport), 2 (energy) and 3 (emissions avoided through reuse).
Yes, our reports comply with ESRS standards, particularly E5 (circular economy). Automatic export in required formats.

rse.q: What is the average reuse rate?
rse.a: On average, 72% of collected equipment is refurbished and reused. The remainder is recycled through certified channels.
Yes, we work with a network of partner charities. Refurbished equipment can be directed to donations with tax receipts.

rse.q: How do you measure avoided emissions?
rse.a: By comparing the carbon impact of reuse vs manufacturing new equipment, using certified Boavizta methodology.

rse.q: Do you provide an annual impact report?
rse.a: Yes, each client receives a detailed annual impact report with carbon metrics, reuse rates, and SDG contributions.

daf.a: Per-asset pricing with volume discounts. No setup fees or hidden subscriptions. Custom quote within 48h.

daf.q: Is there a buyback value for equipment?
daf.a: Yes, refurbishable equipment generates a buyback value deducted from the invoice. Average 15-30% of residual value.
Detailed monthly billing by site and operation type. Accounting export compatible with SAP, Sage and Cegid.
Our clients see an average 200% ROI over 12 months through asset recovery and operational cost reduction.
Flexible 12 to 36-month contracts with exit clause. Option to start with a 3-month no-commitment pilot.

compliance.a: Yes, as a data processor under GDPR. DPA available, up-to-date processing register, designated DPO and supporting certifications.

compliance.q: How do you prove data destruction?
compliance.a: Individual erasure certificate per asset with SHA-256 hash, eIDAS timestamping and blockchain recording for irrefutable proof.
Yes, we facilitate audits: read-only platform access for auditors, pre-formatted reports and dedicated support.
Our certified erasure process meets NIS2 requirements for essential and important entities. Compliance documentation available.

compliance.q: How do you handle data transfers outside the EU?
compliance.a: No transfers outside the EU. French hosting, exclusively European subcontractors, GDPR compliance contractually guaranteed.

ip.text: All content on this website (text, images, videos, logos, trademarks) is protected by intellectual property law. Any reproduction, representation or distribution, in whole or in part, without prior written authorization from GreenTechCycle is prohibited.

liability.title: Liability
liability.text: GreenTechCycle strives to ensure the accuracy of information published on this website but cannot be held responsible for errors, omissions or results obtained from the use of this information.

object.title: Article 1 — Purpose
object.text: These Terms of Use define the conditions for accessing and using the greentechcycle.fr website and the GreenTechCycle platform.

access.title: Article 2 — Website Access
access.text: The website is freely accessible to any user with internet access. Access to the GreenTechCycle platform requires creating a user account subject to validation.

account.title: Article 3 — User Account
account.text: Users agree to provide accurate information when creating their account. They are responsible for the confidentiality of their credentials and all activity performed from their account.

usage.title: Article 4 — Service Usage
usage.text: Users agree to use the platform in accordance with its intended purpose and applicable laws. Any abusive, fraudulent or inappropriate use may result in account suspension.

ip.title: Article 5 — Intellectual Property
ip.text: All platform elements (code, design, content, trademarks) are the exclusive property of GreenTechCycle. Users have a limited right of use within the scope of their contract.

liability.title: Article 6 — Liability
liability.text: GreenTechCycle implements all reasonable means to ensure platform availability and security. GreenTechCycle's liability is limited to direct and foreseeable damages.

termination.title: Article 7 — Termination
termination.text: GreenTechCycle reserves the right to suspend or terminate platform access in case of Terms violation, after prior notification except in emergencies.

law.title: Article 8 — Applicable Law
law.text: These Terms are governed by French law. Any dispute shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Lyon.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.text: GreenTechCycle SAS ('GreenTechCycle') is committed to protecting the privacy of users of its website and platform. This policy describes the personal data collected and how it is processed.

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.text:
GreenTechCycle SAS
42 rue de la République, 69002 Lyon
DPO: dpo@greentechcycle.fr

data.title: Data Collected
data.text:
We collect the following data:
• Identification data: name, surname, email, phone
• Professional data: company, position, sector
• Connection data: IP address, access logs, cookies
• Usage data: platform actions, preferences

purpose.title: Processing Purposes
purpose.text:
Your data is processed for:
• Providing and improving our services
• Client relationship management
• Sending commercial communications (with consent)
• Compliance with legal obligations
• Platform security

legal.title: Legal Basis
legal.text:
Data processing is based on:
• Contract performance (service delivery)
• Consent (cookies, newsletter)
• Legitimate interest (security, service improvement)
• Legal obligation (accounting records)

retention.title: Retention Period
retention.text:
• Active client data: contract duration + 3 years
• Prospects: 3 years after last contact
• Connection data: 12 months
• Accounting data: 10 years

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.text:
Under GDPR, you have the following rights:
• Right of access to your data
• Right to rectification
• Right to erasure
• Right to data portability
• Right to object
• Right to restriction of processing

To exercise your rights: dpo@greentechcycle.fr

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.text: Please refer to our dedicated cookie policy for more information on cookie usage.

transfers.title: Data Transfers
transfers.text: No data is transferred outside the European Union. All our subcontractors are located within the EU and subject to strict contractual clauses.

update.title: Updates
update.text: This policy may be updated. The date of last update is indicated at the top of the page. Last update: April 2026.

intro.title: What is a cookie?
intro.text: A cookie is a small text file placed on your device when visiting a website. It allows the site to remember information about your visit.

necessary.title: Strictly Necessary Cookies
necessary.text: Essential for site operation. Cannot be disabled. Examples: session, language preferences, security.

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.text: Allow us to measure site traffic and improve its performance. Tool used: Matomo (hosted in France).

functional.title: Functional Cookies
functional.text: Enhance your experience (live chat Crisp, display preferences). Can be disabled without affecting basic functionality.

marketing.title: Marketing Cookies
marketing.text: Used for advertising tracking and retargeting. Only placed with your explicit consent.

management.title: Managing Your Preferences
management.text: You can change your preferences at any time by clicking the 'Cookie settings' link at the bottom of the page or through your browser settings.

duration.title: Retention Period
duration.text: Cookies are stored for a maximum of 13 months in accordance with CNIL recommendations.

NotFound.title: Page Not Found
NotFound.subtitle: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.

hero.title: Client use cases
hero.subtitle: 8 sector scenarios delivered in 2024-2025 — each with hard numbers: tCO₂e avoided, € recovered, audit days saved, % compliance reached.

cases.title: Windows 11 migration and NIS2 compliance on 2,400 trading-floor workstations
cases.context: Tier-1 European bank, fleet of 2,400 trading-floor workstations to migrate to Windows 11 by 14 October 2025 (W10 end of support). Banking supervisor audit scheduled for March 2026, NIS2 in force since 18 October 2024, DORA in force since 17 January 2025. No formalised chain of custody until then, paper erasure certificates scattered.
cases.problem: The CISO must prove to the regulator that every decommissioned asset has been erased to NIST 800-88, transported under sealed chain of custody, destroyed or refurbished by a vendor listed in the DORA TPP register. Without this proof, supervisor sanction up to 1% of consolidated revenue.
cases.stakeholders: CISO, Workplace CIO, DPO, DORA Compliance, Operational risk director, Procurement
cases.solution: IT Fleet module connected to ServiceNow CMDB; NIST 800-88 Purge erasure with 4-eyes; eIDAS-sealed chain of custody and qualified RGS** timestamping; pre-configured DORA art. 28 + NIS2 + supervisor matrix; GTC Forensic Audit Pro Service for pre-audit; signed PDF/XBRL export to the supervisor.
cases.results: 2,400 workstations processed in 11 weeks (vs initial 6-month plan) · €187k residual value recovered · 312 tCO₂e avoided (Boavizta v1.4) · supervisor pre-audit cleared in 4 days instead of 3 weeks · 100% of certificates eIDAS-signed within 24 h.
cases.testimonialQuote: The eIDAS proof closed every supervisor question. For the first time, our CISO sleeps easy on a DORA audit.
cases.testimonialRole: IT Security Director, Tier-1 European bank (CAC40)

cases.title: Refurbishment and erasure of 800 medical-imaging drives without disrupting care
cases.context: Regional public teaching hospital, 9,000 beds, end-of-life medical imaging fleet (MRI, CT, ultrasound). 800 HDDs and SSDs hold patient DICOM imaging subject to 20-year retention and certified destruction. Health-data-hosting certification mandates physical destruction for any HDS-classified medium.
cases.problem: Continuity of care prohibits any examination interruption. Drives must be extracted, removed from the HDS perimeter, destroyed per national health-data agency standard, with chain of custody documented for a data protection authority audit. The DPO must sign a destruction protocol per asset without halting any operating block.
cases.stakeholders: Biomedical CIO, DPO, Quality & risk director, CISO, Sterilisation pharmacists, Regional health agency
cases.solution: On-site Audit & Inventory Pro Service with biomedical accompaniment; NIST 800-88 Purge erasure in CCTV-monitored zone; HMG IS5 Enhanced 6 mm shred; auto-generated HDS protocols; non-HDS IT assets refurbished and donated through a social inclusion partner.
cases.results: 800 drives destroyed in 9 days · 0 operating-block interruption · 100% protocols signed within 48 h · €142k residual value (refurbished admin workstations) · 84 tCO₂e avoided · 0 data protection incident since rollout.
cases.testimonialQuote: The HDS-aware module replaces three separate vendors. Our DPO now has the traceability the regulator has been expecting since 2022.

cases.title: First ESRS E5 disclosure and value recovery on 1,800 OT assets
cases.context: French industrial mid-cap, 4,500 employees, 11 production sites, first CSRD ESRS E5 disclosure due 30 June 2026. Mixed OT fleet (Siemens S7 PLCs, Schneider HMIs, Beckhoff industrial PCs) end-of-life, scope 3.1 dominating the carbon footprint. CSR director under pressure from the statutory auditor.
cases.problem: ESRS E5 §29-§39 requires auditable data: resource inflows, outflows, circularity rate, hazardous waste. No internal tool computes these 16 data points. The 4 historical ITAD vendors deliver 4 untraceable Excel formats.
cases.stakeholders: CSR Director, CFO, Management control, Industry 4.0 CIO, Statutory auditor, EHS Director
cases.solution: Carbon module scope 3.1/3.7/3.11 calibrated GHG Protocol + Boavizta v1.4; automatic mapping of 16 ESRS E5 data points; XBRL/PDF export auditable to ISAE 3410; CSRD Reporting Pro Service with statutory-auditor accompaniment; OT asset value recovery via specialist refurbished market.
cases.results: 1,800 OT assets processed · €248k residual value (vs €0 prior systematic destruction) · 421 tCO₂e avoided · 11 of 16 ESRS E5 data points produced automatically · ESRS E5 audit cleared in 6 days by the statutory auditor.
cases.testimonialQuote: We thought ESRS E5 readiness would take 18 months. With GTC we were ready in 4 months and the statutory auditor signed off without reservations.

cases.regulation: Public procurement + circular-economy law + restricted distribution
cases.title: Digital sovereignty and social-inclusion clause across 1,800 ministerial workstations
cases.context: Mid-size French government department, 1,800 workstations at the end of a 36-month lease. Procurement requires a social-inclusion clause and proof of digital sovereignty (100% EU subcontractors). Some assets are classified restricted distribution.
cases.problem: Reconcile three contradictory requirements: financially recover the fleet, guarantee data sovereignty, honour the social-inclusion clause. Standard vendors fail on at least one of the three.
cases.stakeholders: Secretariat-General, CISO, Ministerial DPO, Procurement, Digital Director, Social-inclusion partner workshop
cases.solution: Platform hosted on EU sovereign cloud, EU-only subcontractors; T2 secure transport with restricted-distribution clearances for IGI 1300 perimeter; refurbishment delegated to a social-economy partner with inclusion reporting; quarterly value-and-impact export.
cases.results: 1,800 workstations processed · 73% reinjected into social-economy circuit (1,314 units) · 41 inclusion-track jobs supported · €96k residual value returned to the public treasury · 0 restricted-distribution asset outside the sovereign perimeter · 261 tCO₂e avoided.
cases.testimonialQuote: We ticked all three boxes: sovereignty, value recovery, inclusion. No other vendor was able to offer it.

cases.title: WakiBox rollout and pooled collection across 220 stores in 90 days
cases.context: French retail brand, 220 stores, triennial renewal of POS and payment terminals. 850 POS, 1,600 payment terminals, 4,200 electronic shelf labels (ESL) to retire. PCI-DSS v4.0 in force since 31 March 2025 mandates certified destruction of assets carrying EMV certificates.
cases.problem: Coordinate 220 sites in 90 days without commercial disruption, handle heterogeneous hardware (POS, payment terminals, ESL, peripherals), produce a PCI-DSS protocol per site and a consolidated CSRD report. Regional managers resist any operational friction.
cases.stakeholders: Retail CIO, CISO, DPO, Operations Director, 220 store managers, CSR Director
cases.solution: 6-wave rollout (37 stores/wave, 14 days); deployment of 84 WakiBox IoT kiosks for pooled accessory/ESL collection; PCI-DSS Appendix A1 erasure; physical destruction of payment terminals in sealed zone; 3-year POS refurbished to EMEA market; CSRD ESRS E5 reporting per brand.
cases.results: 220 sites processed in 87 days (vs 90 budgeted) · 0 commercial disruption · 6,650 assets retired · €412k residual value (POS + back-office servers) · 198 tCO₂e avoided · 100% PCI-DSS protocols delivered within 72 h.
cases.testimonialQuote: The WakiBox kiosks saved 2 h/week to every store manager. And we finally have a PCI-DSS proof opposable to the acquirer.
cases.testimonialRole: IT Operations Director, French retail brand 220 stores

cases.title: Decommissioning of 4,200 smart-meter concentrators with sealed chain of custody
cases.context: French energy operator, NIS2 essential entity, fleet of 4,200 G1 smart-meter concentrators obsolete (vendor EOSL). Each concentrator holds power-line communication cryptographic keys and sensitive hardware identifiers. National cybersecurity audit scheduled within 30 days.
cases.problem: Erase or destroy cryptographic modules, prove end-to-end chain of custody on 4,200 assets across 38 technical sites, deliver a report compliant with the grid operator's OT security baseline, without slowing field crews.
cases.stakeholders: OT CISO, Network Operations Director, Regulatory Compliance Director, Procurement, National audit office
cases.solution: ITAD Cybersecurity Pro Service: on-site extraction by restricted-distribution-cleared technician; class T2 secure transport; NIST 800-88 Purge erasure of SAM modules; HMG IS5 Enhanced 6 mm shred; eIDAS-sealed chain of custody + qualified RGS** timestamping; national auditor export + IEC 62443 / NIS2 matrix.
cases.results: 4,200 concentrators processed in 38 days · 100% sealed chain of custody · 0 cybersecurity incident · national audit cleared in 9 days · 78 tCO₂e avoided · €0 residual value (mandatory destruction).
cases.testimonialQuote: The eIDAS-sealed chain of custody avoided a reservation in the national audit. Without GTC we would have had 6 weeks of remediation.

cases.title: 12-rack datacenter exit (1,100 servers) with 18-month refurbished resale
cases.context: French telecom operator, planned closure of a colocation site: 12 full racks, 1,100 servers (Dell PowerEdge R740/R750, HPE ProLiant Gen10), 220 Cisco Nexus switches, 60 SAN bays. All assets are 3-5 years old, refurbished-market residual value estimated at €600k+.
cases.problem: Erase SSDs/HDDs to NIST 800-88 Purge, dismantle racks without damaging resaleable components, certify the chain to reassure the refurbisher buyer, optimise the calendar against a €18k/month datacenter occupancy cost.
cases.stakeholders: Infrastructure CIO, Procurement, CFO, IT asset manager, CISO, Logistics
cases.solution: Asset Intelligence Pro Service for EMEA refurbished-market valuation; NIST 800-88 Purge erasure in sealed zone; rack-by-rack dismantling with SKU inventory; eIDAS chain of custody; resale via partner refurbisher network; per-batch financial reporting for the CFO.
cases.results: 1,100 servers + 220 switches + 60 SAN bays processed in 24 days · €638k residual value recovered (vs €78k scrap metal) · 1,247 tCO₂e avoided (refurbished vs new) · €36k datacenter occupancy savings.
cases.testimonialQuote: GTC funded the entire operation and produced €560k of net cash. Our CFO wants this method generalised across all our site closures.
cases.testimonialRole: IT Asset Manager, French telecom operator

cases.title: Solidarity transfer of 3,200 student-grant laptops with 0 data leakage
cases.context: French university, 28,000 students, laptop-loan programme for grant students. 3,200 Lenovo / HP laptops at end of 4-year lease. The university wants to transfer part of the fleet to local associations, student welfare and elementary schools in disadvantaged areas, rather than destroying everything.
cases.problem: Erase all student data (theses, research, personal data) to NIST 800-88, certify the solidarity transfer for the public records authority, produce social-clause reporting for the supervising ministry, without overburdening the IT department.
cases.stakeholders: University CIO, DPO, Student Life Director, Territorial Partnerships Director, Public procurement, Supervising ministry
cases.solution: On-site Audit & Inventory; NIST 800-88 Purge erasure with per-asset proof; refurbishment with open-source education OS preinstalled; transfer via partner social-economy platform; social-clause + CSRD reporting; transfer trace for the records authority.
cases.results: 3,200 laptops processed in 32 days · 78% transferred via solidarity circuit (2,496 units) · 11 partnerships activated · 0 GDPR incident · 312 tCO₂e avoided · documented social-impact valuation for the supervising-ministry report.
cases.testimonialQuote: For the first time our laptops end up in disadvantaged-area schools instead of a skip. The supervisory report turned into a political asset.

unified.title: Why an all-in-one platform?
unified.subtitle: Compare: 4 fragmented tools vs 1 unified platform

cta.title: Your case isn't listed?
cta.subtitle: Describe your fleet in 5 lines: our consultants will get back within 48 h with a comparable case study and a proposed action plan.

hero.title: ITAD for Financial Services
hero.subtitle: DORA, PCI-DSS, regulatory oversight — secure your critical IT asset decommissioning

context.title: Context
context.description: The financial sector handles the most sensitive data: trading positions, client information, regulatory filings. DORA mandates digital operational resilience since 2025, including control over ITAD supply chains. Regulators demand flawless traceability.

challenges.title: Typical challenges
items: Complete audit trail required by regulators
ITAD providers subject to same DORA requirements

solution.title: How GTC responds
items: NIST 800-88 Purge/Destroy certified erasure with dual validation
Built-in 4-eyes principle for every critical operation
Auditor reporting with dedicated regulatory exports
DORA Article 28 compliant ITAD provider qualification

hero.title: ITAD for Healthcare
hero.subtitle: Health data hosting, patient records, privacy — protect health data end-to-end

context.title: Context
context.description: Healthcare facilities manage particularly sensitive patient data. Health Data Hosting (HDS) certification strictly governs the processing of this data, including its destruction. Data protection authorities are especially vigilant in this sector, with regular audits and enhanced penalties.

challenges.title: Typical challenges
items: Patient data: medical records, imaging, lab results
Mandatory physical destruction for certain media (CDs, tapes, disks)
Connected medical devices with embedded data
Multi-site: hospitals, clinics, care homes, practices
Constrained IT budget and aging equipment

solution.title: How GTC responds
items: Certified erasure and physical destruction for all media types
Certificates compliant with health data hosting requirements
Refurbishable equipment recovery through charitable channels

hero.title: ITAD for Industry
hero.subtitle: PLCs, SCADA, industrial IoT — manage the lifecycle of your IT/OT equipment

context.title: Context
context.description: Industry uses a mix of traditional IT and OT (Operational Technology): programmable logic controllers, SCADA systems, IoT sensors. These devices have long lifecycles (10-20 years) and often contain sensitive production data. IT scope 3 emissions are particularly heavy in manufacturing.

challenges.title: Typical challenges
items: Heterogeneous fleets: PLCs, SCADA, HMI, industrial PCs
EOSL (End of Service Life) equipment without vendor support
Heavy scope 3 carbon (bulky equipment, rare metals)
Production data and intellectual property embedded in devices

solution.title: How GTC responds
items: Unified IT/OT inventory with PLC and SCADA management
Erasure adapted to industrial media (flash memory, PLCs)
Precise carbon footprint including heavy equipment via Boavizta
EOSL lifecycle management with alerts and replacement planning
Certified recycling of rare metals and specialized components
Automated CSRD/ESRS E5 reporting for industrial scope 3

hero.title: ITAD for the Public Sector
hero.subtitle: Public procurement, WEEE standards, enhanced GDPR — transparency and compliance for government

context.title: Context
context.description: The public sector (local authorities, government agencies, public institutions) faces enhanced obligations for transparency, GDPR compliance, and public procurement rules. WEEE management standards apply specifically. Procurement specifications are strict and purchasing procedures are governed by public procurement law.

challenges.title: Typical challenges
items: Public procurement: strict specifications, competitive bidding
GDPR with increased public servant accountability
Limited budget, need for maximum value recovery

solution.title: How GTC responds
items: Platform compliant with public procurement requirements
Transparent and auditable reporting for oversight bodies
GDPR certificates compliant with public sector recommendations
Maximum value recovery through charitable reuse channels
Dashboard adapted for government with budget tracking

hero.title: ITAD for Energy & Utilities
hero.subtitle: DORA, NIS2, grid operators, energy code — secure end-of-life for OT/IT critical infrastructure

context.title: Context
context.description: Energy operators (generation, transmission, distribution) run IT/OT assets classified as critical infrastructure operators (CIO) or essential services operators (NIS2). Decommissioning smart-meter concentrators, SCADA RTUs, control rooms or IT firewalls requires a tracked chain of custody, IGI 1300-grade destruction for restricted-distribution assets, and documented CSRD scope 3 reporting. DORA applies to energy entities operating in financial perimeter (trading, hedging).

challenges.title: Typical challenges
items: Volumes: 4,000 to 50,000 OT assets (concentrators, RTUs, IEDs)
12-20 year lifecycles, frequent EOSL from vendors
Sensitive embedded data: network topology, cryptographic keys
Mandatory physical destruction for HSMs, industrial SIMs, SAM modules
Multi-site: control centres, HV/MV substations, smart meters
Cybersecurity authority audit expected within 30 days

solution.title: How GTC responds
items: Unified IT/OT inventory with critical-infrastructure / restricted-distribution tagging
NIST 800-88 Purge erasure + HMG IS5 Enhanced physical destruction (6 mm shred)
eIDAS-sealed chain of custody + qualified RGS** timestamping
Carbon scope 3.1 module calibrated on Boavizta v1.4 energy factors
T2/T3 secure transport with restricted-distribution clearances

hero.title: ITAD for Retail & Distribution
hero.subtitle: PCI-DSS v4.0, GDPR, CSRD — manage end-of-life for POS terminals, devices and multi-site fleets

context.title: Context
context.description: Multi-site retail brands (300 to 3,000 stores) handle payment card data (PCI-DSS) and GDPR-regulated customer data. The triennial renewal of POS systems, payment terminals, electronic shelf labels and interactive kiosks generates heavy and heterogeneous ITAD flows, to be coordinated without commercial disruption. CFOs require actuarial asset valuation and CSRD ESRS E5 reporting per brand.

challenges.title: Typical challenges
items: 300 to 3,000 sites to orchestrate within 90 days
POS systems containing SAM cards, EMV certificates, after-sales data
Payment terminals to be destroyed per PCI-DSS Appendix A1
Interactive kiosks, RFID/ESL labels, digital safes
Pressure from franchisees / store managers on cost and lead time
Consolidated CSRD reporting across brands / countries

solution.title: How GTC responds
items: Multi-site rollout plan in waves with real-time monitoring
PCI-DSS-grade secure erasure + physical destruction with per-site protocol
WakiBox module for pooled collection (ESL, accessories, peripherals)
Asset Intelligence for EMEA refurbished-market valuation
CSRD ESRS E5 reporting per brand, per country, per wave
Store API connected to the TMS to avoid disrupting operations

hero.title: Sectors we serve
hero.subtitle: 6 sector-specific ITAD playbooks — each vertical has its regulators, lifecycles and chain-of-custody constraints.

index.intro: We have documented 6 actionable sector playbooks. Each sector page covers: regulatory context, applicable regulations, typical field challenges, and the GTC operational response (modules, services, SLAs).
